title: Tree recurrence and tangentiality interface
summary: Builds finite sums of left-comb words and proves their tangentiality once a RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality principle is supplied together with a recurrent coefficient system.

mathematicalContent.heading: Finite tree-word operator
mathematicalContent.body: A supported coefficient function A on degree-k left-comb indices defines a rank-r operator by summing the associated nested Laplacian words.

mathematicalContent.heading: Missing analytic principle
mathematicalContent.body: The key commutator theorem is isolated as an explicit proposition: every coefficient system satisfying the path recurrence produces an operator tangential at the chosen multiweight.

mathematicalContent.heading: Certified package
mathematicalContent.body: TangentialOperatorData stores the finite sum, its recurrence proof, and the recurrence-to-tangentiality principle; Lean then derives the advertised tangentiality theorem.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores finite support and a coefficient function for degree-k rank-r left-comb words.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} (D : OperatorData r k) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forms the finite ambient operator sum carried by the tree data.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r k : ℕ} (D : OperatorData r k) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ I ∈ D.support, D.coeff I * I.toOperator CalConf F U
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the finite tree-word operator pointwise.

correspondence.leanType: structure RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r k : ℕ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages the unproved analytic principle that the rank recurrence cancels all Q-defects and implies tangentiality.

correspondence.leanType: theorem recurrence_implies_tangential {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (H : RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality CalConf r k w) (D : OperatorData r k) (hrec : RankRecurrence CalConf.n w D.coeff) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) w
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Applies a supplied recurrence-to-tangentiality principle to a recurrent finite tree-word sum.

correspondence.leanType: structure TangentialOperatorData (r k : ℕ) (w : Fin r → ℝ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores operator data, a recurrence proof for its coefficients, and the required tangentiality principle.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (D : TangentialOperatorData CalConf r k w) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Extracts the ambient operator from a recurrence-certified tree data set.

correspondence.leanType: theorem tangential {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (D : TangentialOperatorData CalConf r k w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights D.toOperator w
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Derives tangentiality of the extracted operator from the stored recurrence and principle.

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: The theorem is conditional on RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality, whose sole field is precisely the missing commutator/defect-cancellation implication. No proof of that analytic principle, output homogeneity, self-adjointness, or arbitrary-tree invariance occurs here.
